name: Szechuan Green Beans & Pork
recipeIngredient: 1 teaspoon Rice vinegar, or white vinegar
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine soy sauce, chicken broth, sherry, sesame oil, vinegar (or white vinegar), sugar, salt, and hot red pepper; set aside.
2. In a wok or 12-inch skillet, heat vegetable oil until very hot but not smoking.
3. In two batches, fry green beans over high heat, turning often with kitchen tongs, until bright green and just beginning to brown, about 2 minutes.
4. Transfer the beans to paper towels to drain. Discard the oil remaining in the wok.
5. Reduce heat to medium. Add pork, garlic, and ginger and cook, stirring to break up lumps of meat, until pork loses its pink color, about 3 minutes.
6. Add green beans and stir-fry for 1 minute longer. Stir in the reserved sauce and cook until the liquid is almost evaporated, about 1 minute.
7. Transfer to a serving dish and sprinkle scallion over top.
This recipe also works well if you use 2-3 tablespoons of oil in a nonstick wok or pan.
